quince-flounder-lezzet.name: Quince Flounder

ingredients.item: 1 kg of lingu (you can also use ankle or shield fish)

instructions: 1. Hurricane in the shape of the bird without peeling the bark of the moon.
2. Heat the olive oil into the pot and jealous fire.
3. Add the moon and hang it for 10 minutes.
4. In addition to the fish meats you eat with lemon juice, fine chopped garlic and bird.
5. Close to pear, fine chopped pepper, fresh thyme, chopped dill, add one pepper and salt.
6. After 15 minutes, take it from the stove and sprinkle with red pepper.
7. If the fish dishes that support mental development appeal to your taste, take a look at the recipe of fish in the light and tasty pan.

quince-liqueur-argiro.name: Quince Liqueur
instructions: You must choose mature quinces without blows. Wash the quinces very well to remove all the fluff from the skin. Wipe them with a towel. We'll use the peel on the liquor as well as the hearts of the quinces.
Cut the quinces in half and then each piece in 3-4 slices.
Add the quince slices with their peel into the glass jar.
Add the arboriza, lemon peel and cinnamon.
Pour the quince with the cognac.
Seal the glass jar well. Keep the quince liqueur in a cool cabinet for 1.5 to 2 months.
When it passes this time of extraction, strain the quince liqueur into a folded tulpan or coffee filter, in a large jug.
We're preparing the syrup. Place in a pot the sugar, water, orange and lemon peels and cinnamon wood.
Mix once and boil for 2-3 minutes after the syrup is boiled.
Lower the syrup from the fire. Let the syrup cool completely.
Remove the peels and cinnamon.
Pour the cold syrup into the quince liqueur.
Mix the liquor. We equalize it to two sterile bottles.
Seal the bottles and let them stand for 1-2 weeks.
I always keep it in the fridge and serve it cool and aromatic after a meal.

quinoa-and-edamame-salad.name: Quinoa And Edamame Salad

ingredients.item: Salt and ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Make the dressing: Combine oil, vinegar, maple syrup, sesame oil, liquid aminos, lemon juice, garlic, red pepper flakes, salt, and pepper in a mason jar. Seal the lid and shake until fully blended.

Make the salad: Combine cooked quinoa, edamame, bell pepper, cucumber, carrot, green onion, and cranberries in a large mixing bowl. Add 1/2 cup of the dressing and mix until well combined; save any remaining dressing for another use. Place in the refrigerator until chilled, at least 1 hour.
Garnished with slivered almonds to serve.
